rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical reductive cross-coupling of α-aryl vinyl bromides with β-alkenyl bromides to construct 1,3-dienes using a sacrificial stainless steel anode that releases Ni catalyst in situ
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of stainless steel anode releases Ni ions into solution; cathodic reduction generates active Ni(0) catalyst B and reduces Ni(II)/Ni(III) intermediates throughout the catalytic cycle

key_intermediates.name: Ni(II) precatalyst A (from anodic dissolution of stainless steel)
Ni(II)-alkenyl bromide complex C (oxidative addition of β-alkenyl bromide)
Ni(I)-alkenyl species D (after cathodic reduction of C)
Ni(III)-bis(alkenyl) bromide species E (after 2nd oxidative addition of α-alkenyl bromide)
Ni(I)-Br species F (after reductive elimination releasing 1,3-diene)

electron_flow: Stainless steel anode is oxidized to release Ni(II) (A), which is cathodically reduced to Ni(0) (B); B performs oxidative addition with β-alkenyl bromide to give Ni(II) (C), then cathodic reduction yields Ni(I) (D); a second oxidative addition with α-alkenyl bromide gives Ni(III) (E), which reductively eliminates the 1,3-diene and Ni(I)-Br (F), which is reduced back to B.

probe_reagents.name: (Z)-(2-bromovinyl)benzene (1m)
probe_reagents.observation: 1:1 mixture of E/Z diene isomers obtained, indicating loss of alkene geometry during the coupling

probe_reagents.name: alkenyl chloride / iodide variants
probe_reagents.observation: Lower yields of 1,3-diene 3d with more homocoupling side products when halide is changed from Br

probe_reagents.name: Pre-electrolysis with SS anode/Ni cathode (no Ni catalyst added)
probe_reagents.observation: ICP-OES detected Fe (552.6 mg/L), Cr (187.1 mg/L), Ni (63.9 mg/L) in solution; subsequent addition of substrates under Gong's protocol gave product 3a in 28% yield, confirming in situ generation of Ni catalyst from electrodes

probe_reagents.name: Cyclic voltammetry
probe_reagents.observation: Supported proposed redox events of Ni species (details in SI)

notes: Notable that no exogenous metal catalyst or chemical reductant is required: Ni is generated in situ from the stainless steel anode (which also contains Fe and Cr by ICP-OES). The Z/E scrambling with 1m suggests a radical or planar alkenyl-Ni intermediate rather than fully stereoretentive oxidative addition. TMSCl additive likely activates the electrode surface or Ni species.
